title: Classifying a Triangle by Its Angles

steps.instruction: How do we classify triangles by angles?
steps.work: Acute: all angles < 90°. Right: one angle = 90°. Obtuse: one angle > 90°.
steps.explanation: Look at the largest angle to classify.

steps.instruction: A triangle has angles 35° and 55°. Find the third angle.

steps.work: The third angle is 90°, so this is a right triangle.
steps.explanation: Having exactly one 90° angle makes it a right triangle.

title: Dual Classification: By Sides and Angles

steps.instruction: A triangle has sides 5, 5, 7 cm and angles 44°, 44°, 92°. Classify by both sides and angles.
steps.work: Sides: two equal (5, 5) → isosceles. Angles: 92° > 90° → obtuse.
steps.explanation: We can classify the same triangle in two ways.
The triangle is described by both its side property (isosceles) and its angle property (obtuse).

steps.work: Two equal sides → two equal angles (44° = 44°). ✓ Angles sum to 44 + 44 + 92 = 180°. ✓
steps.explanation: In any triangle, equal sides are opposite equal angles, and the angle sum is always 180°.
